More Notes: Observed wallaby grass height of 52 cm in GR-7.5M-M in the first observation period. Biochar-related parameters generally did not have a significant impact, except in the case of GRs with fine biochar particles at the top of the substrate (GR-7.5T-F), which failed to increase the height of this plant species.
Common everlasting plants reached up to 33 cm in GR-15M-M.
Maximum height of billy button plants observed during the last two observation periods in GR-7.5M-M.

The average height of Wallaby Grass is observed to vary with treatments, measured for the period from 1/3/2024 to 1/6/2024 in comparison to earlier periods recorded in 2023. Specifically, GR-15M shows a decrease in height to ~28 cm from ~38 cm in previous terms.

Biochar-modified GRs have greater plant coverage area compared to unmodified. GR-15M-M (15% medium biochar mixed) had greatest effect.
Biochar-amended substrates (GR-7.5M-M, GR-15M-M, and GR-7.5M-F) were noticeably lower in temperature than GR-0.
Slight improvement in substrate temperature when biochar was applied at the bottom of the substrate (GR-7.5B-M).
Top-dressed biochar (GR-7.5T-F) increased substrate temperature during the day.
GR-7.5T-F (top-dressed biochar) released absorbed heat more quickly than GR-0 and cooled down faster at night.
The effects of biochar in reducing substrate temperature were more pronounced at higher ambient temperatures.
